title: Stock Market Highlights, Jul 20: Sensex drops 442 pts to end at 77,708, Nifty closes below 24,240; Axis Bank, HDFC Bank fall most - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty closed lower on Monday, dragged down by heavy selling in blue-chip bank stocks HDFC Bank and Axis Bank amid margin-related concerns and a flare-up in US-Iran tensions.

The 30-share BSE Sensex declined 442.93 points, or 0.57 per cent, to settle at 77,708.52. During the day, it tanked 783.16 points, or 1 per cent, to 77,368.29.

The 50-share NSE Nifty edged lower by 95.80 points, or 0.39 per cent, to end at 24,238.50.

Among Sensex shares, Axis Bank was the biggest loser, dropping by 5.48 per cent after its quarterly results over the weekend. HDFC Bank declined by 5.12 per cent.

“HDFC Bank has disappointed, particularly on the NIM front,” V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Investments Limited, said.

Maruti, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Infosys, Tata Consultancy Services, Mahindra & Mahindra and Bharat Electronics were also among the laggards from the blue-chip pack.

Trent, Power Grid, NTPC and Bharti Airtel were among the gainers.

Sovereign Gold Bond investors enjoy 34-44% returns despite gold price corrections, though tax changes affect new investor appeal.

Hyundai Motor India has integrated 30,000 EV charging points into the myHyundai app and plans to expand its fast-charging network to 600 stations by 2030.

The rupee fell to a two-month low as Brent crude hovered near $90 amid Middle East tensions. RBI intervention helped limit losses against the dollar.

Bajaj Life Insurance has launched the Nifty 200 Value 30 Index Fund under its ULIPs, offering policyholders a value investing strategy alongside life insurance protection. Tracking the Nifty 200 Value 30 Index, the fund invests in 30 value-oriented companies selected through a transparent, rules-based methodology. The NFO, open from 14th to 27th July 2026 , is designed for long-term investors seeking disciplined wealth creation.

UltraTech Cement reports 17% profit rise and plans significant capacity expansion by fiscal 2028 amid strong sales volumes.

It has already raised $300 million in FCNR (B) deposits alone as of date.

Tata Elxsi has received the order from the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] in respect of its appeal against the penalty levied under Section 271(1) of the Income Tax Act, amounting to Rs. 67 lakh.

Tata Motors will launch the India-made Nexon as the Tata Osprey in South Africa, highlighting the growing global role of Indian-built vehicles and exports.

Honeywell Aerospace secures avionics deal with IndiGo for 810 A320neo aircraft and runway safety tech for Aeromexico's fleet.

Smartworks leases 1,100 desks to L&T Technology Services in Pune, expecting ₹55 crore rental income over five years.

Aurum PropTech reports ₹45 crore net profit in Q1 2026-27, driven by higher revenue and property sales gains.

Lohia Corp's ₹1,102 crore IPO opens July 23 at ₹404-425 per share, entirely an OFS by existing shareholders.

Total income rose to ₹10,938 crore during the April-June period from ₹8,866.47 crore a year earlier, the bank said in a stock exchange filing

IL&FS Group repays ₹50,387 crore of debt by June 2026, achieving 82.6% of its resolution target amid ongoing asset resolution efforts.

Shares were down 4% to Rs 298.25 on the NSE.

Under this new agreement, DRF will train and facilitate the placement of 1,250 PwDs by March 2028.

Sensex ▼393.42 pts or 0.50% at 77,758.03

Nifty 50 ▼87.95 pts or 0.36% at 24,246.35.

P. Abhushan, Indo Thai Securities, Vimta Labs, Venus Remedies, Dynamic Cables, Aurum Proptech, Tinna Rubber and Infrastructure, Steel Exchange India, Bajaj Healthcare, Advit Jewels, Rajoo Engineers, South West Pinnacle Exploration, CSM Technologies, Regency Fincorp, Swastika Investsmart, Rajshree Sugars and Chemicals, Super Sales India, Alkali Metals, California Software, Anubhav Plast, Hawa Engineers, Swarna Securities, Golden Legend Leasing and Finance, and Decorous Investment & Trading Co

Shyam Metalics reports a 21% increase in Q1 PAT to ₹351 crore, with revenue rising to ₹5,502 crore.

The project was awarded under SECI's tariff-based global competitive bidding for the supply of 1,500 MW / 12,000 MWh of power from pumped hydro storage projects being set up in the country

SEBI has empanelled 18 firms, including major auditors, for forensic audits of listed companies to enhance market transparency.

Markets off the day’s low: Sensex traded 411.23 pts or 0.53% lower at 77,740.22; and Nifty 50 declined 97.50 pts or 0.40% to 24,236.80.

Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL), a Fortune Global 500 company and a Maharatna PSU, today launched Bharatgas Lite ZIP, a premium Free Trade LPG (FTL) offering designed to redefine the customer experience through Instant New Connection and Express Delivery.

Mahindra Logistics reported standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 at Rs 29 crore compared to Rs 6.44 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares jumped 8% to Rs 417.50 on the NSE.

China and India reported the largest decline in liquefied natural gas (LNG) imports in the 2025 calendar year (CY) at 8.9 million tonnes (mt) and 1.5 mt, respectively, according to the International Gas Union (IGU).

Indian Overseas Bank’s standalone net profit for the quarter ended June 2026 increased 49.3% to Rs 1659.24 crore from Rs 1111.04 crore in the same quarter last year.

Shares surged 3% to Rs 34.95 on the NSE.
